afar: adverb. At, to, or from a great distance; far away.
aft: adverb. At, near, or towards the stern of a vessel (with the frame of reference within the vessel).
air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
aircraft: noun. A vehicle capable of atmospheric flight due to interaction with the air, such as buoyancy or lift.
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
arc: noun. That part of a circle which a heavenly body appears to pass through as it moves above and below the horizon.
arf: interjection. Synonym of woof (“sound of a dog's bark”).
aria: noun. A musical piece written typically for a solo voice with orchestral accompaniment in an opera or cantata.
art: noun. The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses
car: noun. A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for
carat: noun. A metric unit of mass equal to exactly 200 mg, chiefly used for measuring precious stones and pearls.
carr: noun. Any marsh; marshy ground, swampland.
cart: noun. A small, open, wheeled vehicle, drawn or pushed by a person or animal, often with two wheels on one axle, more often used for
cat: noun. A mammal of the family Felidae.
fact: noun. Something actual as opposed to invented.
fair: adjective. Beautiful, of a pleasing appearance, with a pure and fresh quality.
fart: verb. To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
fat: adjective. Carrying more fat than usual on one's body; plump; not lean or thin.
fiar: noun. One in whom the property of an estate is vested, subject to the estate of a liferenter.
fiat: noun. An arbitrary or authoritative command or order to do something; an effectual decree.
fir: noun. A conifer of the genus Abies.
frat: noun. Shortened form of fraternity, college organization.
friar: noun. A member of a mendicant Christian order such as the Augustinians, Carmelites (white friars), Franciscans (grey friars) or the
frit: noun. A pre- fused ceramic mixture used to make glass and glazes.
raft: noun. A flat - bottomed craft able to float and drift on water, used for transport or as a waterborne platform.
raia: noun. Alternative form of raya (“type of historical territory”).
rat: noun. Any of the numerous members of several rodent families that usually have short limbs, a pointy snout, a long, hairless tail, and a
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rif: verb. Alternative letter-case form of RIF.
tar: noun. A black, oil, sticky, viscous substance, consisting mainly of hydrocarbons derived from organic materials such as wood, peat, or coal.
tiara: noun. The three- tiered papal crown.
tic: noun. A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
triac: noun. A three- terminal electronic component that conducts current in either direction when triggered; a bidirectional triode thyristor.
ara: noun. The great blue and yellow macaw (Ara ararauna).
artic: noun. Abbreviation of articulated lorry; A semi-trailer truck.
crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
fra: noun. A title of a friar or monk: brother.
fri: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Western Frisian.
raita: noun. A condiment made from seasoned yogurt used as a dip or sauce in the cuisine of southern Asia.
rata: noun. Any of various New Zealand plants of the genus Metrosideros.
taa: noun. A kind of pagoda in China and Japan.
taar: noun. Alternative form of tar (“Persian string instrument”).
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
taraf: noun. A division of land in India, usually a (small) subdivision of a pargana.
tari: noun. A small gold coin, worth a quarter of a dinar, struck by the Arabs of Sicily in the 9th century.
tfr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teribe.
tra: noun. Synonym of iridescent shark (“a shark catfish of species Pangasianodon hypophthalmus”).
trica: noun. An apothecium in certain lichens, having a spherical surface marked with spiral or concentric ridges and furrows.
tricar: noun. three-wheeler; motor vehicle with three wheels
